revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:泉質]]


{{One source|date=2022nd year 3月}}

This article discusses the '''classification of hot springs''' (温泉), referred to as '''senshitsu''' (泉質) in Japanese.

== Definition ==
Hot springs ({{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|qid=Q1365924|lt=mineral springs|1=Mineral spring}}) emerging from the ground contain various dissolved substances. Based on the concentration of 18 specific substances defined by the {{ill|Hot Springs Law|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Springs Law|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} and the total dissolved solids (TDS) per kilogram of spring water, the classification of hot springs is determined according to the {{ill|Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|ja|鉱泉分析法指針|1=Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mineral Spring Analysis Guidelines|13=User:Immanuelle/Guidelines for Mineral Spring Analysis|qq=}}.

The results of the chemical analysis are summarized in what is known as a {{ill|hot spring analysis report|ja|温泉分析書|1=Hot spring analysis report|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring analysis report|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring analysis report|qq=}}, often displayed within facilities. Additionally, the {{ill|Efficacy|en|Efficacy|qid=Q2585693|lt=therapeutic benefits|1=Efficacy}} of hot springs are defined based on their classification (excluding traditional claims without scientific basis).

== Classification of Therapeutic Hot Springs ==
Hot springs classified as {{ill|therapeutic hot springs|ja|療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic hot springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}} are categorized under three main naming conventions: display names, old classification names, and new classification names. The new classification system was introduced in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=Showa 54|1=1979}} to replace the old system, but the old names remain widely used due to their simplicity.

=== Therapeutic Hot Springs ===
The display names for therapeutic hot springs are categorized into 11 types. For a correspondence table of display names and old/new classifications, refer to the external links.
* Simple hot springs - Contain less than 1g of dissolved substances (excluding gases) per kilogram of water, with a temperature of 25°C or higher.
# {{ill|Simple hot springs|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}}

* Saline springs - Contain 1g or more of dissolved substances (excluding gases) per kilogram of water, regardless of temperature.
# {{ill|Carbon dioxide springs|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon dioxide springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}
# {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}
# {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}}
# {{ill|Sulfate springs|ja|硫酸塩泉|1=Sulfate spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}}

* Therapeutic hot springs containing special components - Contain specified concentrations of particular components, regardless of temperature.
# {{ill|Chalybeate|en|Chalybeate|qid=Q3046709|lt=Chalybeate springs|1=Chalybeate springs}}
# {{ill|Alum springs|ja|含アルミニウム泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alum springs|qq=}}
# {{ill|Copper-iron springs|ja|含銅-鉄泉|1=Copper-Iron Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Copper-iron springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Copper-Iron Spring|qq=}}
# {{ill|Sulfur springs|ja|硫黄泉|1=Sulfur spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfur spring|qq=}}
# {{ill|Acidic springs|ja|酸性泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acidic springs|qq=}}
# {{ill|Radioactive spring|ja|放射能泉|1=Radioactive spring|1=Radioactive spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Radioactive spring|qq=}}

=== Non-therapeutic Hot Springs ===
Hot springs that meet the legal definition under the {{ill|Hot Springs Law|ja|温泉法|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|1=Onsen Act|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Springs Law|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Act|qq=}} but do not fit into the above 11 categories are considered "non-therapeutic hot springs." These include springs with an outflow temperature below 25°C and over 1000 mg/kg of dissolved solids, or springs containing metasilicic acid or metaboric acid above the specified concentration but lacking sufficient therapeutic components.

Although they cannot formally display therapeutic benefits, these springs may provide similar {{ill|general therapeutic effects|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/general therapeutic effects|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}} when heated for bathing.

== Classification of Mineral Springs ==

=== Classification by Spring Temperature ===
Mineral springs are classified by their spring temperature (temperature at the point of emergence or collection). Low-temperature, standard, and high-temperature springs are collectively termed hot springs.
# Cold springs - Below 25°C
# Low-temperature springs - 25°C to below 34°C
# Hot springs - 34°C to below 42°C
# High-temperature springs - 42°C and above

=== Classification by Acidity ===
Based on the {{ill|PH|en|PH|qid=Q40936|lt=pH|1=PH}} of the water at emergence:
# Acidic - pH below 3
# Weakly acidic - pH 3 to below 6
# Neutral - pH 6 to below 7.5
# Weakly alkaline - pH 7.5 to below 8.5
# Alkaline - pH 8.5 and above

=== Classification by Osmotic Pressure ===
Based on total dissolved solids (TDS) or freezing point:
# Hypotonic - TDS below 8g/kg, freezing point -0.55°C or higher
# Isotonic - TDS 8 to below 10g/kg, freezing point below -0.55°C to -0.58°C or higher
# Hypertonic - TDS 10g/kg or higher, freezing point below -0.58°C
==Non-therapeutic categorizations==
These categorizations are not based on therapeutic effects but rather on things like appearance or location

*{{ill|Moll spring|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moll spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}}
*{{ill|Black hot spring|ja|黒湯|1=Kuroyu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}}
*{{ill|Red hot spring|ja|赤湯|1=Red Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Spring|qq=}}
*{{ill|Cloudy hot spring|ja|にごり湯|1=Murky Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cloudy hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Murky Hot Springs|qq=}}

* {{ill|Red springs|ja|赤湯|1=Red Spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Spring|qq=}}

== External Links ==
* {{PDFlink|[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei.pdf Comparison Table of Old and New Hot Spring Classifications]}} - Ministry of the Environment
* [https://www.bathclin.co.jp/kenkoukarada/onpro/effect/quality.htm Hot Spring Quick Reference Table] - Bathclin Corporation
* [https://www.spa.or.jp/onsen/501/ Various Types of Hot Spring Classifications] - Japan Spa Association

{{Onsen-stub}}
